title: cronsplain schedule tournament VERDICT: score A/B via vectors, promote winner to lib/schedule.js + tests/test_schedule.js
description: Verifier-role tournament verdict (Co-Scientist consensus). Scores both candidates against the candidate-agnostic golden vectors, promotes the winner to the shipped lib/schedule.js, and writes its test. Depends on T-046 (vectors harness) + T-047 + T-048 (both candidates). PRODUCES lib/schedule.js -> must be handed off to a DIFFERENT verifier identity (producer != approver); F6: the approver must differ from every producer in the epic.
acceptance_criteria: OWNS EXACTLY: projects/cronsplain/lib/schedule.js, projects/cronsplain/tests/test_schedule.js. Touch no candidate file (read-only) and no other file.

SCORE: require candidates/vectors.js (runAgainst) + candidates/schedule_a.js + candidates/schedule_b.js. Run runAgainst on BOTH candidates. Record each candidate's passed/total + failures in the handoff note (replayable). Pick the winner by pass-rate, then on a tie by clarity/robustness/edge-case handling. Document the rationale in the handoff note AND a header comment in lib/schedule.js naming which candidate won and why + the verdict date.

PROMOTE by COPY: lib/schedule.js contains the winner's implementation as the canonical shipped module (export nextOccurrences per the vectors interface). It MUST NOT require() anything from candidates/ at runtime (promotion = copy, so the shipped lib has zero candidates/ dependency). module.exports = { nextOccurrences }.

tests/test_schedule.js (node:test): (1) require('../candidates/vectors').runAgainst against require('../lib/schedule').nextOccurrences and assert passed===total; (2) PLUS >=2 explicit assertions written independently of vectors.js — one DOM/DOW-OR case (e.g. '0 0 13 * 5') and one EXCLUSIVITY case (from exactly on a match returns the NEXT minute).

PRODUCER != APPROVER + F6: hand off to a verifier whose identity differs from every producer (T-044..T-048, T-050, T-051). Do not self-verdict. The final join (T-052) re-runs test_schedule.js against the promoted file.
SHARED CONVENTIONS: CommonJS, zero deps, UTC. Run from projects/cronsplain/.
HARNESS PROTOCOL: claim/lock(lib/schedule.js, tests/test_schedule.js)/in_progress/handoff --to-role verifier; NEVER self-mark done; --agent <you>.
VERIFY (in handoff note): from projects/cronsplain/ `node --test tests/test_schedule.js` green; include both candidates' runAgainst scores + the winner rationale.

notes.note: Judging tournament: re-run self-checks 11/11 each, score on impossible-schedule behavior + perf + clarity + safety-cap, run own discriminating probes, promote one winner verbatim to lib/schedule.js, write tests/test_schedule.js.

Artifacts: promoted lib/schedule.js (candidate B verbatim, self-check dropped, zero candidates/ require) + tests/test_schedule.js (6 tests). Full suite from projects/cronsplain: node --test tests/*.js = 63 pass 0 fail (57 prior + 6 new).

HANDOFF -> verifier: TOURNAMENT VERDICT T-049 (cronsplain schedule). WINNER = candidate B (field-cascade carry, candidates/schedule_b.js / T-048) promoted VERBATIM to projects/cronsplain/lib/schedule.js. LOSER = candidate A (brute-force minute tick, T-047). SCORE TABLE (re-run by tournament-judge from projects/cronsplain): (1) golden runAgainst: A 11/11, B 11/11, zero failures both. (2) impossible schedule 0 0 30 2 wildcard: A returns empty array silently (len 0), B THROWS documented Error naming the cause -- B better for a user-facing next command. (3) safety-cap semantics: A caps TOTAL minutes scanned (MAX_MINUTES_TO_SCAN=6,000,000 min, ~11.4 yr) so on a LEGIT sparse schedule 0 0 1 1 wildcard count 20 A silently truncates to 11; B caps day-carries BETWEEN hits (SAFETY_CAP_DAYS=3660) and returns all 20 -- B correct. (4) performance sparse leap 0 0 29 2 wildcard from 2023 count 2 (200 iters): A ~91ms vs B ~0.095ms = ~960x. (5) exclusivity/dense-1000/mid-hour-seam probes: A and B agree and are both correct. RATIONALE: outputs are equivalence-proven on satisfiable within-horizon inputs so the verdict turned on out-of-overlap behavior; B fails loudly on impossible schedules where A returns a silent partial, B's cap bounds the inter-occurrence gap (the right semantic) where A's total-horizon cap silently truncates legitimate long sparse sequences (reproduced 0 0 1 1 wildcard count 20 -> A 11 / B 20), and B is ~960x faster on sparse schedules; both are 11/11 conformance-clean so B wins decisively with no blending. LOSER STRENGTHS (gen-4): candidate A is simpler and obviously-correct (tick-and-test on plain UTC getters, no carry-logic edge cases) -- an ideal independent oracle for future cross-diff of this module. PROMOTION FIDELITY: lib/schedule.js body byte-identical to schedule_b except two error strings renamed schedule_b: -> schedule:; zero runtime require of candidates/ (grep: only comment mentions); module.exports = { nextOccurrences }; self-check guard dropped. TESTS: tests/test_schedule.js (node:test, 6 tests) = golden 11/11 via candidates/vectors.runAgainst against ../lib/schedule, plus hand-written DOM/DOW-OR (0 0 13 wildcard 5), exclusivity (next-minute), leap (0 0 29 2 wildcard), impossible-throws, long-horizon-sparse. REPLAY from projects/cronsplain: node candidates/schedule_b.js (self-check 11/11); node --test tests/test_schedule.js (6 pass); node --test tests/*.js (63 pass 0 fail = 57 prior + 6). F6: producer=tournament-judge; a verifier whose identity differs from tournament-judge and every T-044..T-051 producer must verdict.

VERIFIED (verifier-d, independent replay + cognitive diversity via AST/body-diff + out-of-overlap probes; distinct from tournament-judge and all producers substrate-worker-1/2/3). (1) PROMOTION FIDELITY: difflib body-diff of lib/schedule.js vs candidates/schedule_b.js over SAFETY_CAP_DAYS..module.exports = 189/189 lines IDENTICAL except EXACTLY the two error-string renames 'schedule_b:'->'schedule:' (throw sites); require.main self-check guard dropped; grep require/candidates in lib = only comment mentions, ZERO runtime require of candidates/. No blending. (2) DECISIVE CLAIMS REPRODUCED against PROMOTED lib: '0 0 1 1 *' from 2026-01-01 count=20 -> B returns 20 (2027..2046, all Jan-1 00:00, strictly ascending), A returns 11 (2027..2037); '0 0 30 2 *' -> B THROWS ('cronsplain schedule: no valid day found within 3660 day-carries...'), A returns [] silently len 0; exclusivity '*/15' from 00:15 -> 00:30; leap '0 0 29 2 *' from 2023 count 2 -> 2024,2028; runAgainst(promoted)=11/11 (A also 11/11). (3) TESTS: node --test tests/test_schedule.js=6/6; node --test tests/*.js=63/63 (57 prior+6); the 6 pin impossible-throws, long-horizon-sparse(len20 2027..2046), DOM/DOW-OR, exclusivity x2, leap, golden 11/11; test-time require('../candidates/vectors') permitted by acceptance criterion 4, shipped lib is candidate-free. (4) 450-vs-count20 RECONCILED: A[0..10]===B[0..10] byte-identical within A reach; divergence is ONLY occurrences 12-20 (2038..2046) BEYOND A's 6,000,000-min (~11.4yr) TOTAL cap -> verifier-c's 450-input cross-diff stayed in-horizon (0 divergences TRUE) while judge probed OUTSIDE the overlap (truncation TRUE); neither lied. Perf re-timed: A 90.83ms/call vs B 0.098ms/call = ~928x (~judge's 960x). Winner justified: tie 11/11 -> tiebreak on robustness/edge/clarity; loser-strength recorded (A=simple oracle) in note AND lib header. (5) PROCESS/F6: events.jsonl - judge locked lib+test @05:40:11 BEFORE write @05:42:25, handoff->verifier @05:43:05 (NO self-done, zero status=done by tournament-judge), released @05:43:18; verifier-d never produced any cronsplain file. ACCEPT.
